Indonesia’s 1%: 50 Richest as Wealthy as 55 Million People
body: Inequality is widening in Indonesia, a country home to over 280 million people, as only a few business elites control the nation’s wealth.

title: JCI Opens in the Red as US–Iran Hopes Unravel, MSCI Holds Back Upgrade
body: JCI falls 0.41% to 7,528 as US–Iran optimism fades, while MSCI keeps Indonesia’s status unchanged despite reform progress.

title: Albanese Praises Indonesia as Urea Exports Begin
body: Australia thanked Indonesia for a 250,000-ton urea export deal as Jakarta expands fertilizer sales abroad.

title: Indonesia to Allow China to Issue Sovereign Bonds Domestically
body: Indonesia had previously announced plans to issue Panda Bonds in China, citing the country’s competitive interest rates.

title: UN Says Indonesia to Grow 4.9% This Year, Malaysia at 4.4%
body: The UN ESCAP says that Indonesia's real GDP growth is expected to ease from 5.1% in 2025 to 4.9% the following year.

title: Economists Warn Limited Easing Room as BI Set to Hold 4.75%
body: Economists expect BI to hold rates at 4.75% as rupiah risks, oil volatility and global uncertainty narrow room for easing.

title: Plastic Costs - Not Supply - Behind Higher Cooking Oil Prices, Minister Says
body: The price of subsidized cooking oil brand Minyakita has surpassed the government’s maximum retail price of Rp 15,700 per liter.

title: Indonesia Declines Multi-Billion-Dollar Loan Offers from IMF and World Bank
body: The IMF reportedly offered a credit facility ranging between $25 billion and $35 billion.

title: JCI Falls 0.46% as MSCI Limits, Oil Risks Weigh on Sentiment
body: JCI fell 0.46% to 7,559 with Rp 17.7 T turnover as MSCI restrictions and US-Iran ceasefire risks cloud sentiment.

title: Indonesia’s Pertamina Clarifies All-Indian Crew in Hormuz-Trapped Vessel
body: Pertamina recently drew public backlash after a social media influencer revealed that its vessel had recruited an all-Indian crew.

title: Indonesia, Poland Push Beef, Dairy Trade, Eye EU Market Access
body: Indonesia and Poland move to fast-track beef and dairy trade while exploring EU market access to strengthen food security.

title: IDX Steps Up MSCI Talks as Index Curbs Remain
body: MSCI keeps curbs in its May review while assessing new ownership data, as IDX steps up engagement to sustain investor confidence.

title: Toyota Invests $76 Million for CATL-Backed Battery Project in Indonesia
body: From bioethanol plant to hybrid batteries, Toyota is ramping up its investment in Indonesia.

title: Indonesian Stocks Edge Down on MSCI Restrictions, Oil Risks
body: Stocks open in the red as MSCI restrictions persist and oil climbs on fresh US-Iran tensions, clouding outlook.

title: MSCI Keeps Indonesia Stocks on Hold as Reform Review Continues
body: MSCI keeps Indonesia under index curbs, awaiting proof that reforms improve transparency and free float ahead of a June review.

title: Fuel, LPG Hikes Squeeze Middle Class, Risk Subsidy Overrun
body: Analysts warn fuel, LPG hikes may hit middle-class spending and push 5–10% users into subsidized LPG, risking quota breach.

title: Non-Subsidized Fuel Prices May Rise Again if Oil Stays High, Minister Says
body: Indonesia may raise non-subsidized fuel prices if oil stays high, while holding Pertamax steady to prevent a shift to subsidized fuels.

title: Indonesia Announces 5 TCF Gas Discovery in East Kalimantan
body: The discovery was made by global energy firm Eni and also includes an estimated 300 million barrels of oil-equivalent condensate.

title: Rupiah May Slip to 17,200 Next Session: Analyst
body: Rupiah strengthened to Rp 17,168 per US dollar on Monday but is expected to weaken toward 17,200 in the next session, an analyst said.

title: Indonesia Could Forfeit Rp 67 T a Year Without  Windfall Profit Tax, Indef Warns
body: Commodity boom lifts revenue, but Indef says Indonesia still loses massive gains without windfall tax reform.
